Name: module
Description: The name of the vOMIX-MEGA module to execute.

Name: workdir
Description: The working directory for the underlying Snakemake workflow back-end. Modify only for advanced customization or debugging purposes.

Name: basedir
Description: The base directory path hosting the Snakefile for the Snakemake workflow back-end. Modify only for advanced customization or debugging purposes.

Name: outdir
Description: The directory path where structured output results from vOMIX-MEGA will be deposited. New directories will be automatically created (including nested structures). Existing directories will be overwritten or appended.

Name: datadir
Description: The path to raw FASTQ files. Used to verify if files are pre-downloaded or as the target destination for new downloads.

Name: samplelist
Description: The path to the sample_list.csv configuration file for inputs and file paths. For detailed specifications visit https://github.com/holab-hku/vOMIX-MEGA/wiki.

Name: fasta
Description: The path to a single input FASTA file for modules that accept a single file as valid input. File path must end with .fasta,  .fa, or .fna

Name: fastadir
Description: The path to a directory containing input FASTA files. The workflow automatically selects all files within this directory ending with .fasta,  .fa, or .fna

Name: sample-name
Description: The explicit sample name utilized for output file naming when providing inputs via --fasta or config['fasta'].

Name: assembly-ids
Description: A list format specified array (e.g.  ['sampleA',  'SampleB']) mapping sample names to input files when using --fasta-dir or config['fastadir']. Note that this feature is undergoing evaluation.

Name: latest-run
Description: An internal logging parameter designating the timestamp of the current execution for tracking vOMIX-MEGA history inside the .vomix subdirectory. See documentation at https://github.com/holab-hku/vOMIX-MEGA/wiki.

Name: splits
Description: The number of parallel groups to partition data into during processing to reduce memory overhead (e.g.  when running geNomad). A value of 0 disables partitioning.

Name: keep-intermediates
Description: Specifies whether to retain substantial intermediate processing files  such as fastp-cleaned raw FASTQ files prior to host decontamination.

Name: setup-database
Description: Determines whether to initialize or update databases when executing modules other than 'setup-database'. Existing databases will not be re-installed unless forced using Snakemake parameters like --forcerun or -F.

Name: max-cores
Description: The maximum number of CPU cores allocated dynamically across parallel Snakemake tasks. This feature is distinct from execution parameters like -j or -n and is currently in development.

Name: NCBI-email
Description: The user email address provided to NCBI E-utilities for data retrieval and download verification.

Name: NCBI-API-key
Description: The NCBI API key required for higher throughput data retrieval. For setup details  visit https://ncbiinsights.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/2017/11/02/new-api-keys-for-the-e-utilities.

Name: hostile-index-db
Description: The directory path where the Hostile database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: PhaBox2-db
Description: The directory path where the PhaBox2 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: genomad-db
Description: The directory path where the geNomad database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: virsorter2-db
Description: The directory path where the VirSorter2 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: vibrant-db
Description: The directory path where the VIBRANT database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: checkv-db
Description: The directory path where the CheckV database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: iphop-db
Description: The directory path where the iPHoP database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: iphop-db-version
Description: The version identifier for the iPHoP database. Verify database compatibility mapping at https://bitbucket.org/srouxjgi/iphop/src/main/#markdown-header-host-databases-and-versions.

Name: iphop-db-basename
Description: The primary base name of the iPHoP database. Verify compatibility criteria at https://bitbucket.org/srouxjgi/iphop/src/main/#markdown-header-host-databases-and-versions.

Name: eggNOG-db
Description: The directory path where the eggNOG database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: eggNOG-db-params
Description: Configuration parameters supplied for downloading the eggNOG database. For more information  consult https://github.com/eggnogdb/eggnog-mapper.

Name: humann-db
Description: The directory path where the HUMAnN3 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: checkm2-db
Description: The directory path where the CheckM2 database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: GTDBTk-db
Description: The directory path where the GTDB-Tk database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: GTDBTk-db-version
Description: The reference version of the GTDB-Tk database. Ensure that the database version corresponds with the local GTDB-Tk installation environment as detailed at https://ecogenomics.github.io/GTDBTk/installing/index.html#gtdb-tk-reference-data.

Name: dwnld-params
Description: Optional configuration parameters used during raw FASTQ retrieval from NCBI via entrez-fetch.

Name: dwnld-only
Description: Flag specifying whether to restrict execution exclusively to remote SRA file downloads from NCBI without proceeding to pre-processing steps.

Name: pigz-params
Description: Execution parameters passed directly to pigz for multi-threaded compression of downloaded SRA FASTQ data.

Name: fastp-params
Description: Additional runtime arguments supplied to the fastp quality control engine.

Name: hostile-params
Description: Additional runtime arguments supplied to the Hostile host decontamination module.

Name: hostile-index-name
Description: The name identifier of pre-built Hostile indices. Verify availability within the installed Hostile tool version; consult https://github.com/bede/hostile for details.

Name: decontam-host
Description: Flag specifying whether to perform host decontamination post fastp quality trimming. Host indexes are provided via the --hostile-index-name CLI flag or config['hostile-index-name'].

Name: hostile-aligner
Description: The short-read alignment backend algorithm employed for host decontamination (either 'bowtie2' or 'minimap2'). Note that minimap2 is not recommended for short-read sequencing datasets.

Name: hostile-aligner-params
Description: Additional runtime arguments supplied directly to the selected Hostile alignment tool.

Name: assembler
Description: The primary assembly tool engine selected for the assembly module (spades support is currently in development).

Name: megahit-min-len
Description: The minimum contig length threshold used during the MEGAHIT assembly filter steps.

Name: megahit-params
Description: Additional runtime parameters supplied directly to the MEGAHIT execution pipeline.

Name: spades-params
Description: Additional runtime parameters supplied directly to the SPAdes metagenomic assembler execution line.

Name: spades-memory
Description: The upper threshold of RAM memory (in gigabytes) allocated for SPAdes assembly execution.

Name: contig-min-len
Description: The absolute minimum length constraint for contig inclusion within the viral-identify module; shorter sequences are purged from analysis.

Name: genomad-min-len
Description: The minimum contig length evaluated by geNomad; sequences falling below this parameter are excluded from classification steps.

Name: genomad-params
Description: Additional runtime command line arguments supplied to geNomad execution within the viral-identify framework.

Name: genomad-cutoff
Description: The minimal numeric confidence threshold required by geNomad to classify a contig sequence as viral.

Name: genomad-cutoff-s
Description: The minimum confidence threshold applied during geNomad secondary filtering. Setting this to 0 bypasses the secondary filter pipeline entirely.

Name: checkv-original
Description: Flag allowing execution of standard CheckV instead of the lower memory  high-efficiency CheckV-PyHMMER implementation.

Name: checkv-params
Description: Additional operational arguments supplied directly to the CheckV pipeline execution.

Name: clustering-fast
Description: Flag triggering an accelerated  MEGABlast-based clustering protocol optimized for viral operational taxonomic unit (vOTU) compilation.

Name: cdhit-params
Description: Additional operational runtime values supplied directly to the CD-HIT clustering utility.

Name: vOTU-ani
Description: The average nucleotide identity (ANI) clustering percentage threshold used during fast clustering workflows (default 95% per MIUViG guidelines: https://doi.org/10.1038/nbt.4306).

Name: vOTU-targetcov
Description: The minimum target coverage alignment coverage percentage used during fast clustering workflows (default 85% per MIUViG guidelines: https://doi.org/10.1038/nbt.4306).

Name: vOTU-querycov
Description: The target query alignment coverage percentage criteria implemented within the fast clustering protocol.

Name: dvf-min-len
Description: The lower bound contig length cut-off implemented during DeepVirFinder evaluation; shorter contigs are ignored.

Name: phamer-min-len
Description: The lower bound contig length cut-off implemented during PhaMer evaluation; shorter sequences are omitted.

Name: dvf-params
Description: Additional system parameters passed directly to the DeepVirFinder tool environment.

Name: phamer-params
Description: Additional system parameters passed directly to the PhaMer execution process.

Name: virsorter2-params
Description: Additional runtime system configurations passed directly to the VirSorter2 tool framework.

Name: vf-params
Description: Additional runtime parameters supplied directly to the VirFinder diagnostic tool.

Name: vibrant-params
Description: Additional run configurations supplied directly to the VIBRANT functional annotation platform.

Name: seeker-params
Description: Additional configuration variables passed directly to the Seekr deep-learning tool instance.

Name: ppr-params
Description: Additional calculation settings passed directly to the PPR-META diagnostic model pipeline.

Name: dvf-cutoff
Description: The minimal confidence score metric required by DeepVirFinder to classify a sequence as viral.

Name: dvf-pval
Description: The maximum critical p-value threshold permitted by DeepVirFinder to confirm a sequence classification as viral.

Name: phamer-pred
Description: The taxonomic classification category targeted by PhaMer prediction routines.

Name: phamer-cutoff
Description: The minimal confidence threshold value required for a positive viral determination within the PhaMer algorithm.

Name: vf-cutoff
Description: The minimal confidence metric needed to evaluate a sequence as viral via VirFinder scoring.

Name: virsorter2-cutoff
Description: The minimal confidence score cut-off requirement designated for positive viral identification in VirSorter2.

Name: seeker-cutoff
Description: The structural scoring minimal cutoff used by Seeker for positive sequence identification.

Name: ppr-cutoff
Description: The numeric determination cutoff used by PPR-META for assigning viral presence profiles.

Name: vibrant-cutoff
Description: The validation score cutoff applied across the automated metrics inside VIBRANT sequence classification.

Name: phagcn-min-len
Description: The minimum allowed contig length for evaluation using the PhaGCN taxonomic assignment engine.

Name: phagcn-params
Description: Additional operational arguments passed to the PhaGCN classification instance.

Name: genomad-params-tax
Description: Additional operational configurations passed to geNomad during taxonomic assignment stages.

Name: coverm-params
Description: Additional mapping or calculation flags passed to the CoverM coverage engine.

Name: coverm-methods
Description: The calculation metric outputs selected for CoverM. Modify only for pipeline testing or debugging.

Name: iphop-host
Description: Flag indicating whether to perform iPHoP-based viral host prediction instead of CHERRY. Note that iPHoP requires a significantly larger database and high memory allocation.

Name: iphop-cutoff
Description: The minimum confidence threshold required by iPHoP to assign a host classification profile to a viral sequence.

Name: iphop-params
Description: Additional configuration arguments supplied directly to the iPHoP platform interface.

Name: CHERRY-params
Description: Additional execution parameters configured for the CHERRY host prediction algorithm.

Name: PhaTYP-params
Description: Additional custom parameters passed directly to the PhaTYP lifestyle prediction module.

Name: eggNOG-params
Description: Additional execution arguments passed directly to the underlying eggNOG-mapper tool engine.

Name: PhaVIP-params
Description: Additional pipeline configurations supplied directly to the PhaVIP viral pathogenic interaction engine.

Name: mpa-params
Description: Additional execution arguments passed to MetaPhlAn during community profiling stages.

Name: mpa-indexv
Description: The version string of the MetaPhlAn database index. Downloads automatically if missing from the environment.

Name: binning-consensus
Description: Flag enabling a consensus-based metagenomic binning protocol combining MetaBAT2,  MaxBin2,  and CONCOCT via DASTool. Disabling this runs GPU-accelerated VAMB clustering instead.

Name: strobealign-params
Description: Additional alignment flags or scoring rules passed to the strobealign tool backend.

Name: MetaBAT2-params
Description: Additional parameters supplied to customize the MetaBAT2 cluster processing constraints.

Name: MaxBin2-params
Description: Additional configuration rules passed directly to the MaxBin2 mathematical binning model.

Name: CONCOCT-params
Description: Additional runtime parameter sets passed directly to the CONCOCT unsupervised binning script.

Name: jgi-summarize-params
Description: Additional runtime parameters supplied to the jgi_summarize_bam_contig_depth depth processing command.

Name: DASTool-params
Description: Additional scoring parameters provided directly to the DASTool bin optimization script.

Name: checkm2-params
Description: Additional parameter fields provided directly to the CheckM2 bin validation pipeline.

Name: galah-params
Description: Additional execution configurations passed directly to the galah bin dereplication pipeline.

Name: GTDBTk-identify-params
Description: Additional parameter variables supplied directly to the GTDB-Tk identify command execution.

Name: GTDBTk-align-params
Description: Additional parameter variables supplied directly to the GTDB-Tk align command execution.

Name: GTDBTk-classify-params
Description: Additional parameter variables supplied directly to the GTDB-Tk classify command execution.

Name: VAMB-params
Description: Additional sequence and structural arguments supplied to the VAMB neural network cluster step.

Name: humann-params
Description: Additional software parameters directed to the HUMAnN functional annotation pipeline.

Name: virsorter2-annotate-params
Description: Additional configuration parameters passed directly to the VirSorter2 annotation sub-module.

Name: dram-v-annotate-params
Description: Additional operational arguments supplied directly to the DRAM-v sequence annotation pipeline.

Name: dram-v-distill-params
Description: Additional parsing and formatting options passed to the DRAM-v distill visualization process.

Name: dram-db
Description: The directory path where the DRAM database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: dram-setup-params
Description: Operational configurations supplied to initialize build or index the DRAM database environment.

Name: metacerberus-db
Description: The directory path where the MetaCerberus database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: metacerberus-setup-params
Description: Operational configurations supplied to initialize build  or index the MetaCerberus database environment.

Name: metacerberus-params
Description: Additional functional and runtime variables supplied directly to the MetaCerberus annotation pipeline.

Name: pharokka-db
Description: The directory path where the pharokka database is installed or will be downloaded. Defaults to the Snakemake base directory under workflow/databases.

Name: pharokka-params
Description: Additional execution parameters passed directly to the pharokka bacteriophage annotation framework.

Name: phabox2-db-name
Description: The designated database name or identifier file package required for the PhaBox2 classification tool execution.

Name: phabox2-db-baselink
Description: The primary remote server base link URL used to fetch and download resource updates for the PhaBox2 database.
